skill.name: frontend-aesthetics
skill.description: Guides AI assistants to create distinctive frontend visuals with stronger typography, color, motion, and contextual style.
skill.summary: Create less generic frontend interfaces with clearer visual direction.
skill.author: Crossbill-Highlights

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed file contains only frontend design guidance. No executable code, commands, network access, filesystem access, environment access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits are plain design-language text, including the description and aesthetic guidance. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, hashing, encryption, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain natural-language frontend design advice only. There is no code path where a weak cryptographic algorithm could be invoked.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance hits are design instructions about focus areas, patterns, backgrounds, and creative interpretation. They do not request host discovery, system enumeration, command execution, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains no shell commands, OS queries, network probing, or inventory behavior. The suspicious terms are used in visual design context.

content.value_statement: Many AI-built interfaces look generic and interchangeable. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward more specific typography, color, motion, and layout choices.
actual_capabilities: Guides typography choices toward distinctive and context-aware font selections.
Encourages cohesive color themes with CSS variables and sharper accent decisions.
Promotes purposeful motion through CSS animations and React Motion when available.
Suggests layered backgrounds, gradients, and geometric atmosphere for visual depth.
Helps avoid common AI-generated frontend patterns and overused visual defaults.
limitations: It provides design direction, not production-ready component code by itself.
It does not include accessibility testing rules or compliance checklists.
It does not provide brand assets, licensed fonts, or image resources.
It may need project-specific constraints to match an existing design system.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Web App Screen
use_cases.description: Turn a plain generated screen into a more memorable interface with better typography, color, motion, and background treatment.

use_cases.title: Set Creative Direction
use_cases.description: Give an AI assistant stronger visual guidance before it creates a landing page, dashboard, or product interface.

use_cases.title: Review Generated UI
use_cases.description: Identify generic visual choices and replace them with decisions that fit the product context.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Basic Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-aesthetics skill to improve this screen. Focus on typography, color, spacing, and background depth.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Visual Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-aesthetics skill to propose a distinctive visual direction for this product interface before writing code.

prompt_templates.title: Avoid Generic AI Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of an existing AI-generated interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-aesthetics skill to audit this UI for generic AI styling and suggest concrete visual replacements.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Interface System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for cohesive frontend execution.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-aesthetics skill to design a cohesive interface system with typography, color variables, motion rules, and background treatments.

output_examples.input: Improve a generic SaaS dashboard layout.
output_examples.output: A more specific dashboard direction with a stronger type pairing, focused accent color, denser information hierarchy, and subtle panel transitions.

output_examples.input: Make a landing page feel less like a template.
output_examples.output: A revised visual approach with context-specific imagery, distinctive headline type, sharper contrast, and a background treatment tied to the product.

output_examples.input: Review an AI-generated interface for visual sameness.
output: Replace the default font stack with a more intentional type choice.
Reduce predictable purple gradients and choose colors tied to the product context.
Use one purposeful motion moment instead of scattered minor animations.

best_practices: Provide the product context, audience, and brand constraints before asking for visual direction.
Ask for concrete typography, color, motion, and background decisions.
Review generated interfaces against the skill before final implementation.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without explaining the product or target user.
Treating visual novelty as more important than usability or clarity.
Accepting overused fonts, purple gradients, and predictable card layouts without review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants create frontend interfaces with stronger visual identity and fewer generic design defaults.

faq.question: Does it generate code?
faq.answer: The skill gives design guidance. Claude, Codex, or Claude Code may apply that guidance while writing frontend code.

faq.question: Is it only for landing pages?
faq.answer: No. It can guide dashboards, apps, product pages, tools, and other frontend experiences.

faq.question: Does it replace a design system?
faq.answer: No. It should be used with existing brand rules, accessibility standards, and component constraints.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Are there security concerns?
faq.answer: The audit found no executable behavior. Static findings were false positives from design-language text.
